intent: Analyse a CITE-seq dataset (paired RNA + ADT/protein). Use a small public CITE-seq dataset (e.g. 10x's 5k/10k PBMC CITE-seq, or a muon/scanpy built-in if available). Load BOTH modalities (muon/MuData), QC + normalize each (CLR for the ADT, standard for RNA), cluster, and show a UMAP coloured by cluster with a few key ADT markers overlaid.

blocks.text: I'll help you analyze a CITE-seq dataset with paired RNA + ADT. Let me start by finding and loading a public dataset, then work through the full pipeline.

input.query: CITE-seq surface protein ADT CLR normalization

blocks.text: Let me check the available skills:
Good! Now let me plan the analysis before executing:

input.title: CITE-seq (RNA + ADT) end-to-end analysis
input.summary: Load a public 5k/10k PBMC CITE-seq dataset as MuData (paired RNA + ADT), run QC and normalization on both modalities (CLR for ADT, standard log-norm for RNA), perform clustering, and visualize with UMAP colored by cluster and key ADT marker overlays.

steps.title: Load or fetch a public CITE-seq dataset (10x PBMC 5k or similar)
steps.description: Locate a small public CITE-seq dataset (e.g., 10x PBMC), download if needed, and load it as a MuData object with both RNA and ADT modalities.

steps.title: QC filtering on both modalities
steps.description: Calculate and apply QC metrics (n_genes, n_counts, MT%, RIBO%) for RNA; protein detection counts and quality for ADT; filter low-quality cells jointly.

steps.title: Normalize ADT (CLR) and RNA (log-normalize)
steps.description: Apply Centered Log-Ratio (CLR) normalization to ADT; standard log1p + scale for RNA. Store in layers for comparison.

steps.title: RNA feature selection and batch integration prep
steps.description: Select highly variable genes from RNA; optionally center/scale; prepare for clustering.

steps.title: Dimension reduction (PCA + UMAP) on RNA
steps.description: Compute PCA on RNA, then UMAP for visualization.

steps.title: Clustering (Leiden on RNA)
steps.description: Compute neighborhood graph on RNA, run Leiden clustering.

steps.title: Visualization: UMAP by cluster + ADT marker overlays
steps.description: Show UMAP colored by cluster assignment, then overlay 4–5 key ADT markers (e.g., CD3E, CD4, CD8A, CD14, CD19) as expression heatmaps or point colors.

assumptions: Using 10x PBMC 5k CITE-seq or a similar public dataset available via URL or a built-in loader.
Muon/MuData is available for multimodal analysis.
CLR normalization will be used for ADT per best practices (log-transform protein counts).
RNA will use standard Seurat-style log1p + scale normalization.
Leiden clustering on the RNA modality will drive the UMAP.
Key ADT markers are among the standard PBMC proteins (CD3E, CD4, CD8A, CD14, CD19, etc.).
